books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.note: Quick reread of the compounding chapter.

blogs.title: Local-first software
blogs.note: You own your data; sync is optional.

glossary.definition: Finding something good without looking for it; happy accident.
glossary.example: Meeting my cofounder was pure serendipity.
glossary.note: Often contrasted with luck you engineer.

glossary.definition: To commit more strongly to a strategy or opinion.
glossary.example: They doubled down on serverless after the first launch.

glossary.definition: A warning or limitation; something to keep in mind.
glossary.example: One caveat: this only works on HTTPS.

glossary.definition: Having subtle differences or shades of meaning; not black-and-white.
glossary.example: The answer is more nuanced than a simple yes or no.

glossary.definition: Standard reused text or code; generic template material.
glossary.example: I skipped the legal boilerplate and read the pricing section.

glossary.definition: Safe to apply more than once — doing it twice has the same effect as once.
glossary.example: PUT is idempotent; repeating the request won’t create duplicates.

glossary.definition: Seeming to be everywhere; very common.
glossary.example: Smartphones are ubiquitous in cities now.

glossary.definition: Focused on practical results rather than theory or ideals.
glossary.example: We took a pragmatic shortcut to ship on time.
